title: A Battle Song for the Demonstration!
translatorNote: This translation is intended to carry Kalaignar's voice into clear, contemporary English rather than recast the letter as literary or academic prose. It preserves the source's argument, political directness, rhetorical questions, repetition, irony, factual detail, and paragraph order. Names, dates, figures, quotations, and intentional English expressions are retained. Where Tamil idiom cannot be reproduced literally without sounding unnatural, the English follows its sense and rhetorical force without adding claims absent from the source. The Tamil below is a source-checked derived reading copy in which clear OCR character, punctuation, and word-break errors have been repaired. The canonical source file remains unchanged and authoritative. `Udanpirappē` is retained in Tamil transliteration rather than flattened into “brother,” “sister,” or “comrade.” Literally evoking “one born alongside me,” Kalaignar uses it as a distinctive address of shared identity, equality, affection, and solidarity within the movement.
paragraphs: The BJP government has introduced amendments to the Land Acquisition Act enacted by the United Progressive Alliance government in 2013, stripping farmers of their rights and seeking to help multinational industrialists. It promulgated an ordinance on 29 December 2014 and, using its strength in the Lok Sabha, passed it there on 10 March 2015.

The Central government has passed these amendments in the Lok Sabha despite the opposition of the majority of the people and is trying to pass them in the Rajya Sabha as well. We condemn this. We also insist that criminal action be taken against the Minister responsible for a government officer's suicide, that he be arrested immediately, and that the case be handed over to the CBI. I have already called upon the district secretaries of the Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam to coordinate and successfully conduct a massive public demonstration at about 10 a.m. on Friday, 20 March 2015. Secretaries from various districts who have come to meet me and contribute to the election fund have personally explained the arrangements being made for the demonstration on the twentieth.
Udanpirappē,
Social activist Medha Patkar has spoken clearly in an interview about the amendments to the Central government's land-acquisition law.

India is an agricultural country. Sixty per cent of India's people depend on agriculture for their livelihood. In this situation, acquiring agricultural lands without examining the differences among them will shrink the area under cultivation and further damage food production. India may be forced to hold out its hand to other countries for food.

Nowhere does a government forcibly acquire land for a private party's needs. Even when land is acquired with people's consent, governments act only after properly providing landowners with compensation, employment and rehabilitation. Indeed, in many countries, when land is acquired in this way, the government itself pays a monthly allowance to those left without work. Under the law brought in by the BJP government, the very lives of farmers who lose their land and of their families will become a question mark.

Most political parties and public organisations are fighting this BJP decision, which gravely affects farmers and the public. When the UPA government brought this law at the Centre in 2013, it contained constructive provisions for rehabilitation and resettlement of those who sold land and compensation for labourers dependent upon it. Members of Parliament, social activists and people from every section were consulted, and the law was enacted under the UPA government on the basis of consensus. Under that law, acquisition required the consent of 10 per cent of the people in an urban area and 20 per cent in a rural area. It also stipulated that people's consent must certainly be secured if acquired land was to be given to private parties.

But the BJP government's amendment bill has removed important provisions from the earlier law. Without consulting anyone, the Central BJP government has granted exemptions from requirements including Social Impact Assessment and investigation, examination and reporting of the dangers posed by projects and the views of the people, special arrangements for food security, and consent from landowners. With these exemptions, any quantity of land can be acquired without anyone's approval or consent. It can only be regarded as an imperial action.

The 2013 Act required land to be returned to those from whom it was acquired if it had not been used for its stated purpose within five years. The Central BJP government has now amended this to say that, however many years pass, it will not be returned until the project is completed. We must not forget that this creates opportunities for the purpose of acquisition never to be fulfilled and for the land to be diverted to other uses.

Ignoring every objection, the BJP government has now passed the law with further amendments and with the AIADMK's support. Will the great beneficiaries be India's poor farmers? Certainly not. They will be India's wealthiest people!
The UPA government's 2013 Act required the consent of the Gram Sabha. In contempt of the democratic method itself, that provision has now been removed.

Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar, the head of a state government, has himself held a hunger strike against the BJP government's land-acquisition law. He declared there: “Even if the Land Acquisition Bill is passed in Parliament, we will not implement this law in Bihar.” Former Chief Minister and former Union Minister Lalu Prasad Yadav is to lead a rally against the amendments. Social activist Anna Hazare has announced a 1,000-kilometre protest march as a continuation of the struggle he has already begun. The DMK, Congress, Trinamool Congress, Samajwadi Party, Janata Dal (United), Communist Party of India (Marxist), Communist Party of India, Rashtriya Janata Dal and Janata Dal (Secular), among others, marched together in Delhi from Parliament to Rashtrapati Bhavan to register their united opposition.

The BJP's own manifesto for the 2014 general election declared: “Land acquisition is a contentious issue due to the opacity of the land acquisition process. BJP will adopt a ‘National Land Use Policy’, which will look at the scientific acquisition of non-cultivable land, and its development; protect the interest of farmers and keep in mind the food production goals and economic goals of the country. Its implementation would be monitored by the National Land Use Authority, which will work with the State Land Use Authorities to regulate and facilitate land management. The power and functions of the National Land Use Authority would be similar to those of other regulatory bodies.” In complete contradiction of that declaration, proving that the BJP says one thing and does another, it has now brought this amendment before Parliament and passed it with the AIADMK's opportunistic support. It is to condemn this that we demonstrate on the twentieth!

If the Central government is engaged in such anti-people and anti-farmer action, there is no need even to ask about our Tamil Nadu government. Are they not “above” everyone in all this? Their anti-people and anti-democratic actions in Tamil Nadu have therefore surpassed even those of the Central government.

Because of pressure exerted by a Minister then responsible for agriculture, Muthukumarasamy, a senior engineer in Tamil Nadu's Agricultural Engineering Department, took his own life in mental anguish, leaving his family stranded. When the news appeared in the media and caused enormous outrage against the AIADMK government among public servants and the public, they hurriedly removed that Minister from both his party post and the Cabinet. Instead of first arresting and questioning the former Minister, they are now questioning his assistants. A widespread suspicion has therefore arisen that covert efforts are under way to protect the former Minister.

This is not the first time under this regime that employees have died by suicide because of pressure from officials, Ministers and their assistants. There was Palanisamy, an officer in the Housing Board at Erode; Thiagarajan, who served as Tahsildar at Ambasamudram in Tirunelveli district; and Rajendran, an engineer in the Tirunelveli Corporation.
Thirteen thousand People's Welfare Workers were dismissed and sent home. Driven by despair and suffering, fifteen of them died by suicide.

Two hundred visually impaired postgraduate teachers have been conducting road blockades, hunger strikes and other protests at several places in Chennai for nine days, pressing demands that include their immediate appointment as teachers.

The Karnataka government has allocated funds in its budget to construct dams across the Cauvery. That state's Chief Minister calls leaders of all parties and seeks their views. He takes them all to Delhi and meets the Prime Minister and the Union Minister concerned. The AIADMK government, however, thinks its duty is over once it has written a letter to the Central government.

Forgetting its election promise, the Central BJP government is attempting to turn into law a black bill that would break the backbone of farmers. To protect its own selfish interests, the AIADMK has stepped forward to assist the Centre's effort by sacrificing farmers' welfare.

Across Tamil Nadu, wherever one looks, there is bribery, corruption, the collapse of law and order, and administrative disorder. By failing to do what must be done and doing only what must not be done, the government has produced discontent, misery and anger on every front. Thousands of problems march upon us; the inactive government can solve none of them; debt grows by the day and the government itself approaches bankruptcy. Anxiety and tears alone are rising in the land. There is not even a drop of light.

The Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam, a lamp to the home and a servant to the nation, has a duty to show our people the wrongful path taken by the Central and State governments and make them understand it. That is why a massive protest demonstration will be held throughout the state the day after tomorrow, on the twentieth. “If this army is defeated, what army can win?” asked Perarignar Anna. Let the army of the movement gather! Let the voice condemning the Central and State governments echo in every direction!
With affection,
M.K.
18 March 2015
